step.title: Parse Request and Detect Tag
step.goal: Read the user's request that opened the session. Distill it into a one-paragraph restatement and select a CANDIDATE primary tag. The tag is a starting hypothesis — step 5's checkpoint may correct it, and ticket-grill may further refine it once the request's true nature surfaces during Q↔R.

substep.title: Restate the Request
substep.action:
Distill the user's request into one or two sentences. The restatement should: - Use the canonical terms from CONTEXT.md when applicable - Be neutral about scope (do not infer features the user did not state) - Surface ambiguity explicitly ("X may mean A or B; this needs grilling")
Store as {{session.request_restatement}}.

substep.title: Select Candidate Primary Tag
substep.action:
Pick ONE primary tag from the six-tag taxonomy (spec §8). Use these heuristics:
- feature -> new product capability. User wants to add or change observable behavior. - bug -> broken behavior. User reports something works incorrectly. - exploration -> spike / learning goal. User wants to investigate or evaluate. - hotfix -> emergency fix. User signals urgency and incident context. - infrastructure -> setup/config/tooling. Not user-facing behavior; cross-cutting plumbing. - chore -> task scope. Small, well-defined task with no decisions.
Tag selection is a CANDIDATE only. The scope checkpoint in step 5 lets the user redirect. Tag may further evolve during ticket-grill if research surfaces the request's true nature is different (e.g., a "feature" request that's really an exploration because foundational questions are unresolved).
Store as {{session.candidate_tag}}.

substep.title: Slug the Request
substep.action:
Generate a kebab-case slug from the request restatement. Conventions: - Lowercase - Words separated by hyphens - 2-5 words maximum - No filler words (the, a, of) unless they clarify - No tag prefix (the alias-form `ticket-YYYY-MM-DD-{slug}` is added by scaffold-bundle.py)
Examples: - "Add team workspaces with shared task lists" -> team-workspaces - "Fix invoice truncation at 1024 chars" -> invoice-truncation - "Investigate adopting tRPC for the API layer" -> trpc-adoption-spike
Store as {{session.slug}}.

substep.title: Display Tag Candidate

section.title: Why only one primary tag
section.description:
Primary tag selects the depth recipe in subsequent phases. Tags like `feature` mandate full Q↔R/D/S/P depth; `chore` minimizes ceremony. Mixing primary tags conflates depth signals.
Secondary tags (security, performance, refactor, etc.) are free-form metadata for filtering — they do NOT change the recipe. Those are set during ticket-grill or ticket-write, not here.

rules.rule: If the user says "emergency", "production down", "blocking customer", "ASAP" -> hotfix
If the user describes plumbing (CI, build, deployment, dependencies, dev tooling) without user-facing behavior -> infrastructure
If the user describes a small mechanical task ("update the README", "rename this field everywhere") -> chore
Otherwise default to feature; correct at scope checkpoint or during ticket-grill
The restatement is what gets presented at step 5's checkpoint — it must be precise enough for the user to spot misunderstanding
Do not silently add scope. If the user said "team workspaces", do not write "team workspaces with billing integration"
Do not silently subtract scope. If the user said "team workspaces with billing", do not collapse to "team workspaces"
Surface ambiguity explicitly with parenthetical flags: "(unclear whether per-team or per-user)"
The slug becomes part of the bundle directory name AND part of every file's canonical alias for the lifetime of the ticket
It is hard to change later (touches frontmatter aliases, parent links, file paths)
If unsure, pick the noun phrase that names the WORK, not the action verb
